title: US First-Class Postage Stamp (1970)
description: The cost to mail a standard first-class letter anywhere in the United States in 1970.
price_context: A first-class stamp today costs $0.73 — more than 12x the 1970 price.

title: Apple iPod (1st Generation 2001)
description: Apple's first iPod. 5GB storage — 1000 songs in your pocket. Introduced by Steve Jobs on October 23 2001.
price_context: Critics called it overpriced at launch. It went on to sell 450 million units before being discontinued.

title: Average Movie Ticket (1985)
description: The average price of a single movie theater ticket in the United States in 1985.
price_context: The average US movie ticket today costs $13.10 — nearly 4x higher in nominal terms.

title: URANIUM ORE — Radioactive Material
description: A real product sold on Amazon. Low-level radioactive uranium ore samples used for Geiger counter calibration. Ships in a small bag.
price_context: One of the most reviewed novelty science products on Amazon with thousands of sarcastic 5-star ratings.

title: Honus Wagner T206 Baseball Card
description: A 1909-1911 tobacco card featuring Pittsburgh Pirates shortstop Honus Wagner. This PSA 3 copy sold at auction in 2022.
price_context: Wagner reportedly demanded American Tobacco stop producing the card — making surviving copies extraordinarily rare.

title: Hermes Birkin 35 (Togo Leather 2024)
description: A hand-stitched French leather handbag with a multi-year waitlist. Retail price new from Hermes boutique.
price_context: Birkins have outperformed the S&P 500 over the last 35 years as an investment asset.

title: McDonald's Big Mac (1980)
description: An American classic — two all-beef patties on a sesame seed bun. Price from a US McDonald's in 1980.
price_context: A Big Mac today averages $5.29 — nearly a 6x increase in nominal price.

title: Apple iPhone (1st Generation 2007)
description: The original iPhone. 4GB storage. No App Store. Changed everything.
price_context: The 8GB model launched at $599 but Apple cut prices to $399 just 68 days later.

title: Gallon of Whole Milk (1975)
description: A standard gallon of whole milk purchased at a US grocery store in 1975.
price_context: Today a gallon averages $3.80 — milk has roughly doubled in real cost.

title: 1987 Nintendo Entertainment System
description: The console that revived the home video game industry in North America. Came bundled with Super Mario Bros. and Duck Hunt.
price_context: Adjusted for inflation this is about $560 today.

title: Original Sony PlayStation (1995)
description: Sony's first home gaming console. Launched in North America in September 1995 with 3D polygon graphics that changed gaming.
price_context: Adjusted for inflation this is about $610 today. The PS1 sold 102 million units lifetime.

title: Dozen Eggs — US Average (1980)
description: The average retail price for one dozen Grade A large eggs at a US grocery store in 1980.
price_context: A dozen eggs averaged $4.99 in early 2026 — a 6x increase driven partly by avian flu outbreaks.

title: Louis Vuitton Neverfull MM (2024)
description: The best-selling handbag in the world. A classic canvas tote sold at Louis Vuitton boutiques worldwide.
price_context: The Neverfull has increased in price over 300% since its 2007 debut at $595.

title: Horse Head Mask (Amazon)
description: A hyper-realistic latex horse head mask. One of Amazon's most iconic novelty products with tens of thousands of reviews.
price_context: Has spawned countless memes and internet videos since first appearing on Amazon around 2012.

title: US Gallon of Gasoline (1972)
description: The average price for one gallon of regular unleaded gasoline at a US pump in 1972.
price_context: The 1973 oil embargo hit the following year — prices nearly doubled to $0.59 by 1974.

title: 1st Edition Charizard Pokémon Card (PSA 10)
description: A 1999 Base Set 1st Edition Charizard holographic card graded PSA 10 Gem Mint. Sold at auction in 2022.
price_context: Logan Paul wore a PSA 10 1st Edition Charizard around his neck at WrestleMania 2022 — it was worth more than most homes.

title: Apple Macintosh 128K (1984)
description: The original Macintosh personal computer. Introduced by Steve Jobs on January 24 1984 with the famous Super Bowl ad.
price_context: Adjusted for inflation this is about $7500 today — making it an extremely premium product for its era.

title: NYC Subway Fare (1970)
description: The cost of a single subway ride on the New York City Transit system in 1970.
price_context: A single NYC subway ride today costs $2.90 — nearly 10x the 1970 fare in nominal terms.

title: Patek Philippe Nautilus 5711 (2021)
description: A Swiss luxury sports watch discontinued by Patek Philippe in 2021. Retail price at authorized dealers.
price_context: After discontinuation the Nautilus 5711 traded on the secondary market for $100000–$150000 — nearly 4x retail.

title: Nicolas Cage Throw Pillow (Amazon)
description: A throw pillow featuring a close-up photographic print of actor Nicolas Cage's face. A genuine bestseller on Amazon.
price_context: Has maintained a near-perfect review rating for over a decade. Comfort level: unknowable.

title: Motorola DynaTAC 8000X (1984)
description: The world's first commercially available handheld mobile phone. Launched in the United States in 1984.
price_context: Adjusted for inflation this is about $12000 today. Battery life: 30 minutes of talk time.

title: New York Times (Single Copy 1960)
description: The cover price of a single copy of The New York Times newspaper sold at a newsstand in 1960.
price_context: A single print copy of The New York Times costs $4.00 today — an 80x increase over 65 years.

title: Banksy — Devolved Parliament (2019)
description: Oil on canvas painting by anonymous street artist Banksy. Sold at Sotheby's London in October 2019.
price_context: This was a record sale price for Banksy at the time. The painting depicts the UK Parliament filled with chimpanzees.

title: Atari 2600 (1977)
description: The console that brought arcade games into American living rooms. Originally sold as the Atari VCS at launch in 1977.
price_context: Adjusted for inflation this is about $1030 today. The 2600 sold over 30 million units.

title: Costco Hot Dog & Soda Combo (2024)
description: A 1/4 lb all-beef hot dog and 20 oz soda at any Costco food court in the United States.
price_context: Costco has kept this price at exactly $1.50 since 1985 — an almost 40-year price freeze despite inflation.

title: Rimowa Original Cabin Suitcase (2024)
description: A German-engineered aluminum carry-on suitcase. The iconic grooved design has been in production since 1950.
price_context: LVMH acquired Rimowa in 2017 for a reported $640 million and repositioned it firmly in the luxury tier.

title: Snuggie — The Blanket with Sleeves (2008)
description: The blanket with sleeves that launched a thousand infomercials. As seen on TV. One size fits most.
price_context: Snuggie sold over 30 million units in its first few years despite widespread mockery — one of the most successful As Seen on TV products ever.

title: Harvard University Annual Tuition (1980)
description: Annual undergraduate tuition at Harvard University for the 1980-1981 academic year. Not including room and board.
price_context: Harvard's annual tuition in 2024-2025 is $59076 — nearly 10x the 1980 rate even before adjusting for inflation.

title: Michael Jordan 1986 Fleer Rookie Card (PSA 9)
description: Michael Jordan's rookie basketball card from the 1986-87 Fleer set graded PSA 9 Mint. Sold at auction in 2021.
price_context: One of the most iconic sports cards ever produced. PSA 10 copies have sold for over $2 million.

title: Nintendo Game Boy (1989)
description: Nintendo's original handheld gaming console. Launched in Japan in April 1989 and North America in July 1989.
price_context: Adjusted for inflation this is about $230 today. The Game Boy line sold over 118 million units combined.

title: Rolex Submariner (2024)
description: The world's most recognized dive watch. The reference 124060 in Oystersteel. Retail price at an authorized Rolex dealer.
price_context: New Rolex Submariners are virtually impossible to buy at retail — grey market prices run 30-50% above MSRP.

title: McDonald's Coffee (1970)
description: A regular cup of drip coffee ordered at a McDonald's restaurant in the United States in 1970.
price_context: A medium hot coffee at McDonald's costs $1.49 today — a 15x increase in nominal price over 55 years.

title: 5-Pound Gummy Bear (Amazon)
description: A single gummy bear weighing 5 pounds. Equivalent to roughly 1400 regular gummy bears. An actual product with thousands of reviews.
price_context: Reviews frequently describe the experience of eating it as a deeply regrettable decision.

title: Action Comics #1 (1938) — 2014 Auction
description: The first appearance of Superman. Published in June 1938. This near-mint copy sold at Comicconnect in 2014.
price_context: Original cover price: 10 cents. About 50-100 copies are known to exist in any condition.

title: Sony Walkman TPS-L2 (1979)
description: The world's first personal stereo cassette player. Introduced by Sony in Japan in July 1979.
price_context: The Walkman created an entirely new category of personal consumer electronics. Over 400 million units sold across all models.

title: Loaf of White Bread — US Average (1980)
description: The average retail price for a standard loaf of white sandwich bread at a US grocery store in 1980.
price_context: A loaf of white bread today averages $2.25 — about 4x higher in nominal terms over 45 years.

title: Cartier Love Bracelet — Yellow Gold (2024)
description: An 18-karat yellow gold bangle with signature screw motifs. Sold at Cartier boutiques worldwide. Requires a screwdriver to put on.
price_context: Cartier introduced the Love Bracelet in 1969 as a symbol of devotion — the screws were meant to keep it locked on.

title: Inflatable Unicorn Horn for Cats (Amazon)
description: A cone-shaped inflatable unicorn horn attached to an elastic chin strap. Designed for cats. Sold on Amazon.
price_context: Frequently purchased with dignity and returned without it.

title: Andy Warhol — Shot Sage Blue Marilyn (2022)
description: A 1964 silkscreen portrait of Marilyn Monroe by Andy Warhol. Sold at Christie's New York in May 2022.
price_context: The highest price ever achieved for an American work of art at auction at the time of sale.

title: Commodore 64 (1982)
description: The best-selling personal computer model of all time. Launched in January 1982 at the Consumer Electronics Show.
price_context: The C64 sold between 12 and 17 million units. Adjusted for inflation its launch price is about $1900 today.

title: Coca-Cola from a Vending Machine (1975)
description: The price of a single 12 oz can of Coca-Cola from a vending machine in the United States in 1975.
price_context: Coca-Cola famously held its vending machine price at $0.05 for 70 years — from 1886 to 1959.

title: Chanel Classic Flap Bag — Medium (2024)
description: The most iconic luxury handbag in the world. Medium size in quilted lambskin leather with gold hardware.
price_context: In 2010 the same bag retailed for $2850. Chanel has raised prices over 300% in 15 years.

title: Full-Body Inflatable Dinosaur Costume (Amazon)
description: A wearable inflatable T-Rex costume powered by a small battery fan. A genuine bestseller and Halloween staple.
price_context: Went viral in 2015 after a woman wore one to her sister's gender reveal party and the footage spread worldwide.

title: 1952 Topps Mickey Mantle Card (PSA 9)
description: The most iconic baseball card in the hobby. Mickey Mantle's 1952 Topps card graded PSA 9 Mint. Sold at Heritage Auctions in 2022.
price_context: Set the all-time record for a baseball card at auction. The 1952 Topps set was Mickey Mantle's first major Topps card.

title: Super Nintendo Entertainment System (1991)
description: Nintendo's 16-bit console and successor to the NES. Launched in North America in August 1991.
price_context: Adjusted for inflation this is about $460 today. The SNES sold 49 million units worldwide.

title: Hershey's Milk Chocolate Bar (1972)
description: The price of a standard Hershey's 1.55 oz milk chocolate bar at a US retailer in 1972.
price_context: A standard Hershey bar today costs $2.19 — a 22x increase. The bar has also been quietly shrunk over the decades.

title: Rolls-Royce Phantom (2024)
description: The flagship ultra-luxury sedan from Rolls-Royce Motor Cars. Base price before options at a US dealership in 2024.
price_context: The average Rolls-Royce Phantom leaves the factory with over $100000 in options added by its owner.

title: Wolf Urine Deer Repellent (Amazon)
description: 100% pure wolf urine. Sprayed around gardens and property lines to deter deer and other prey animals. Sold on Amazon.
price_context: Works by triggering the prey animal's instinctive fear response. Genuinely effective according to many reviewers.

title: Leonardo da Vinci — Salvator Mundi (2017)
description: A painting attributed to Leonardo da Vinci depicting Christ as Savior of the World. Sold at Christie's New York in November 2017.
price_context: The most expensive painting ever sold at auction. Its current whereabouts are unknown — it has not been publicly displayed since 2017.

title: Sega Genesis (1989)
description: Sega's 16-bit home console and direct competitor to the Super Nintendo. Launched in North America in August 1989.
price_context: The Genesis console war with the SNES is considered one of the fiercest in gaming history.

title: Cup of Coffee at a US Diner (1975)
description: The price of a regular cup of drip coffee at a typical American diner in 1975 — free refills included.
price_context: A diner coffee today typically costs $2.50–$4.00. Adjusted for inflation the 1975 price would be about $1.50 today.

title: Bentley Bentayga V8 (2024)
description: A hand-built British ultra-luxury SUV. The Bentayga is the best-selling Bentley in the brand's history.
price_context: Each Bentayga takes approximately 130 hours to build by hand at the Crewe factory in England.

title: The Accoutrements Yodeling Pickle (Amazon)
description: A battery-powered plastic pickle toy that yodels when you press its button. A beloved Amazon novelty item since 2012.
price_context: Has over 5000 five-star reviews. Frequently given as a gag gift. Genuinely yodels.

title: Ferrari 250 GTO (2018 Auction)
description: A 1962 Ferrari 250 GTO — widely considered the greatest sports car ever made. Sold at Sotheby's in August 2018.
price_context: Only 39 Ferrari 250 GTOs were ever built. This sale set the world record for a car sold at public auction.

title: Texas Instruments TI-83 Plus (1999)
description: The graphing calculator that became required equipment in virtually every American high school math class.
price_context: Texas Instruments has sold the TI-83 Plus largely unchanged since 1999. The street price is still around $100-$130.

title: US First-Class Postage Stamp (1985)
description: The price to mail a standard first-class letter anywhere in the United States in 1985.
price_context: The first-class rate rose from $0.06 in 1970 to $0.22 in 1985 to $0.73 today.

title: Christian Louboutin So Kate Pumps (2024)
description: The signature 120mm stiletto heel pump that defines the Christian Louboutin brand. Sold at boutiques worldwide.
price_context: Christian Louboutin began painting his soles red in 1992 after noticing his assistant painting her nails red.

title: Canned Whole Chicken (Amazon)
description: A fully cooked whole chicken preserved in a can. Ready to eat. A real product with a devoted and confused fanbase on Amazon.
price_context: Reviews are a cultural treasure. Common themes include the phrase what is this and photos taken in dim lighting.

title: Polaroid OneStep Camera (1977)
description: The original instant point-and-shoot camera that popularized the iconic white-framed Polaroid photo format.
price_context: Adjusted for inflation this is about $155 today. Polaroid filed for bankruptcy in 2001 but the brand was revived.

title: Gallon of Gasoline — US Peak Price (2008)
description: The all-time average price for a gallon of regular unleaded gasoline at US pumps. Reached in June 2008 during the oil price surge.
price_context: The 2008 spike was driven by speculation and surging demand from China. Prices collapsed to $1.68 by December 2008.
